verses.kjv: ¶ Though he be fruitful among his brethren, an east wind shall come, the wind of the Lord shall come up from the wilderness, and his spring shall become dry, and his fountain shall be dried up: he shall spoil the treasure of all pleasant vessels.
verses.bsb: Although he flourishes among his brothers, an east wind will come— a wind from the LORD rising up from the desert. His fountain will fail, and his spring will run dry. The wind will plunder his treasury of every precious article.

verses.esv: Samaria shall bear her guilt, because she has rebelled against her God; they shall fall by the sword; their little ones shall be dashed in pieces, and their pregnant women ripped open.
verses.kjv: Samaria shall become desolate; for she hath rebelled against her God: they shall fall by the sword: their infants shall be dashed in pieces, and their women with child shall be ripped up.
verses.bsb: Samaria will bear her guilt because she has rebelled against her God. They will fall by the sword; their little ones will be dashed to pieces, and their pregnant women ripped open.

verses.esv: Return, O Israel, to the LORD your God, for you have stumbled because of your iniquity.
verses.kjv: O ISRAEL, return unto the Lord thy God; for thou hast fallen by thine iniquity.
verses.bsb: Return, O Israel, to the LORD your God, for you have stumbled by your iniquity.

verses.esv: Take with you words and return to the LORD; say to him, “Take away all iniquity; accept what is good, and we will pay with bulls the vows of our lips.
verses.kjv: Take with you words, and turn to the Lord: say unto him, Take away all iniquity, and receive us graciously: so will we render the calves of our lips.
verses.bsb: Bring your confessions and return to the LORD. Say to Him: “Take away all our iniquity and receive us graciously, that we may present the fruit of our lips.

verses.esv: Assyria shall not save us; we will not ride on horses; and we will say no more, ‘Our God,’ to the work of our hands. In you the orphan finds mercy.”
verses.kjv: Asshur shall not save us; we will not ride upon horses: neither will we say any more to the work of our hands, Ye are our gods: for in thee the fatherless findeth mercy.
verses.bsb: Assyria will not save us, nor will we ride on horses. We will never again say, ‘Our gods!’ to the work of our own hands.   For in You the fatherless find compassion.”

verses.esv: I will heal their apostasy; I will love them freely, for my anger has turned from them.
verses.kjv: ¶ I will heal their backsliding, I will love them freely: for mine anger is turned away from him.
verses.bsb: I will heal their apostasy; I will freely love them, for My anger has turned away from them.

verses.esv: I will be like the dew to Israel; he shall blossom like the lily; he shall take root like the trees of Lebanon;
verses.kjv: I will be as the dew unto Israel: he shall grow as the lily, and cast forth his roots as Lebanon.
verses.bsb: I will be like the dew to Israel; he will blossom like the lily and take root like the cedars of Lebanon.

verses.esv: his shoots shall spread out; his beauty shall be like the olive, and his fragrance like Lebanon.
verses.kjv: His branches shall spread, and his beauty shall be as the olive tree, and his smell as Lebanon.
verses.bsb: His shoots will sprout, and his splendor will be like the olive tree, his fragrance like the cedars of Lebanon.

verses.esv: They shall return and dwell beneath my shadow; they shall flourish like the grain; they shall blossom like the vine; their fame shall be like the wine of Lebanon.
verses.kjv: They that dwell under his shadow shall return; they shall revive as the corn, and grow as the vine: the scent thereof shall be as the wine of Lebanon.
verses.bsb: They will return and dwell in his shade; they will grow grain and blossom like the vine. His renown will be like the wine of Lebanon.

verses.esv: O Ephraim, what have I to do with idols? It is I who answer and look after you. I am like an evergreen cypress; from me comes your fruit.
verses.kjv: Ephraim shall say, What have I to do any more with idols? I have heard him, and observed him: I am like a green fir tree. From me is thy fruit found.
verses.bsb: O Ephraim, what have I to do anymore with idols? It is I who answer and watch over him. I am like a flourishing cypress; your fruit comes from Me.

verses.esv: Whoever is wise, let him understand these things; whoever is discerning, let him know them; for the ways of the LORD are right, and the upright walk in them, but transgressors stumble in them.
verses.kjv: Who is wise, and he shall understand these things? prudent, and he shall know them? for the ways of the Lord are right, and the just shall walk in them: but the transgressors shall fall therein.
verses.bsb: Whoever is wise, let him understand these things; whoever is discerning, let him know them. For the ways of the LORD are right, and the righteous walk in them but the rebellious stumble in them.
